  geom_boxplot() +
  #geom_point(aes(color = TREATMENT)) +
  geom_jitter(aes(colour = CONCENTRATION), shape=16,size = 2, position=position_jitter(0.2)) +
  ylim(0, 10) +
  facet_wrap(TREATMENT~Phylum, ncol =5) +
  theme_bw() +
  theme(text = element_text(size = 20)) +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = -80, hjust = 0, vjust=0.5)) +
  ggtitle("Relative abundance of selected Phylum") +
  ylab("Relative abundance")
dev.off()



# Plot  
# x = "", color ="", , facet~wrap and comparison list can be modify
pdf("Selected_Phylum_MAX_5_ABUND_Sample_type_DETAILED_FACET_TREATMENT.pdf", width = 30, height = 40)
ggplot(some_Phylum, aes(x = Sample_type_DETAILED, y = Abundance)) +
  geom_boxplot() +
  #geom_point(aes(color = TREATMENT)) +
  geom_jitter(aes(colour = CONCENTRATION), shape=16,size = 2, position=position_jitter(0.2)) +
  ylim(0, 5) +
  facet_wrap(TREATMENT~Phylum, ncol =5) +
  theme_bw() +
  theme(text = element_text(size = 20)) +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = -80, hjust = 0, vjust=0.5)) +
  ggtitle("Relative abundance of selected Phylum") +
  ylab("Relative abundance")
dev.off()



# Plot  
# x = "", color ="", , facet~wrap and comparison list can be modify
pdf("Selected_Phylum_MAX_1_ABUND_Sample_type_DETAILED_FACET_TREATMENT.pdf", width = 30, height = 40)
ggplot(some_Phylum, aes(x = Sample_type_DETAILED, y = Abundance)) +
  geom_boxplot() +
  #geom_point(aes(color = TREATMENT)) +
  geom_jitter(aes(colour = CONCENTRATION), shape=16,size = 2, position=position_jitter(0.2)) +
  ylim(0, 1) +
  facet_wrap(TREATMENT~Phylum, ncol =5) +
  theme_bw() +
  theme(text = element_text(size = 20)) +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = -80, hjust = 0, vjust=0.5)) +
  ggtitle("Relative abundance of selected Phylum") +
  ylab("Relative abundance")
dev.off()



# Plot  
# x = "", color ="", , facet~wrap and comparison list can be modify
pdf("Selected_Phylum_MAX_01_ABUND_Sample_type_DETAILED_FACET_TREATMENT.pdf", width = 30, height = 40)
ggplot(some_Phylum, aes(x = Sample_type_DETAILED, y = Abundance)) +
  geom_boxplot() +
  #geom_point(aes(color = TREATMENT)) +
  geom_jitter(aes(colour = CONCENTRATION), shape=16,size = 2, position=position_jitter(0.2)) +
  ylim(0, 0.01) +
  facet_wrap(TREATMENT~Phylum, ncol =5) +
  theme_bw() +
  theme(text = element_text(size = 20)) +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = -80, hjust = 0, vjust=0.5)) +
  ggtitle("Relative abundance of selected Phylum") +
  ylab("Relative abundance")
dev.off()


###################################
#END OF TEST PLOT ONLY SPECIFIC Phylum
###################################




###################################
#TEST PLOT ONLY SPECIFIC Family
###################################


#some_Family <- physeq_filtered.rel
#some_Family <- tax_glom(some_Family, "Family")
#tax_table(some_Family)[,6]

#some_Family <- subset_taxa(some_Family, Family == "Enterobacteriaceae"| Family == "Oscillospiraceae" 
                     #   |Family == "Lachnospiraceae" | Family == "Coriobacteriaceae"| Family == "Fusobacteriaceae" |
                      #    Family == "Streptococcaceae" | Family == "Sutterellaceae" | Family == "Ethanoligenenaceae" 
                       #  | Family == "Morganellaceae" | Family == "Erysipelatoclostridiaceae" | Family == "Enterococcaceae"
                        # )
#some_Family <- psmelt(some_Family)

# Plot  
# x = "", color ="", , facet~wrap and comparison list can be modify
pdf("Selected_Family_NO_SCALE_Sample_type_DETAILED_FACET_TREATMENT.pdf", width = 40, height = 40)
ggplot(some_Family, aes(x = Sample_type_DETAILED, y = Abundance)) +
  geom_boxplot() +
  #geom_point(aes(color = TREATMENT)) +
  geom_jitter(aes(colour = Sample_type_DETAILED), shape=16,size = 2, position=position_jitter(0.2)) +
  #ylim(0, 5) +
  facet_wrap(TREATMENT~Family, ncol =11) +
  theme_bw() +
  theme(text = element_text(size = 20)) +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = -80, hjust = 0, vjust=0.5)) +
  ggtitle("Relative abundance of selected Family") +
  ylab("Relative abundance")
dev.off()


# Plot  
# x = "", color ="", , facet~wrap and comparison list can be modify
pdf("Selected_Family_MAX_10_ABUND_Sample_type_DETAILED_FACET_TREATMENT.pdf", width = 40, height = 40)
ggplot(some_Family, aes(x = Sample_type_DETAILED, y = Abundance)) +
  geom_boxplot() +
  #geom_point(aes(color = TREATMENT)) +
  geom_jitter(aes(colour = Sample_type_DETAILED), shape=16,size = 2, position=position_jitter(0.2)) +
  ylim(0, 10) +
  facet_wrap(TREATMENT~Family, ncol =11) +
  theme_bw() +
  theme(text = element_text(size = 20)) +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = -80, hjust = 0, vjust=0.5)) +
  ggtitle("Relative abundance of selected Family") +
  ylab("Relative abundance")
dev.off()



# Plot  
# x = "", color ="", , facet~wrap and comparison list can be modify
pdf("Selected_Family_MAX_5_ABUND_Sample_type_DETAILED_FACET_TREATMENT.pdf", width = 40, height = 40)
ggplot(some_Family, aes(x = Sample_type_DETAILED, y = Abundance)) +
  geom_boxplot() +
  #geom_point(aes(color = TREATMENT)) +
  geom_jitter(aes(colour = Sample_type_DETAILED), shape=16,size = 2, position=position_jitter(0.2)) +
  ylim(0, 5) +
  facet_wrap(TREATMENT~Family, ncol =11) +
  theme_bw() +
  theme(text = element_text(size = 20)) +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = -80, hjust = 0, vjust=0.5)) +
  ggtitle("Relative abundance of selected Family") +
  ylab("Relative abundance")
dev.off()



# Plot  
# x = "", color ="", , facet~wrap and comparison list can be modify
pdf("Selected_Family_MAX_1_ABUND_Sample_type_DETAILED_FACET_TREATMENT.pdf", width = 40, height = 40)
ggplot(some_Family, aes(x = Sample_type_DETAILED, y = Abundance)) +
  geom_boxplot() +
  #geom_point(aes(color = TREATMENT)) +
  geom_jitter(aes(colour = Sample_type_DETAILED), shape=16,size = 2, position=position_jitter(0.2)) +
  ylim(0, 1) +
  facet_wrap(TREATMENT~Family, ncol =11) +
  theme_bw() +
  theme(text = element_text(size = 20)) +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = -80, hjust = 0, vjust=0.5)) +
  ggtitle("Relative abundance of selected Family") +
  ylab("Relative abundance")
dev.off()



###################################
#END OF TEST PLOT ONLY SPECIFIC Family
###################################





###################################
#TEST PLOT ONLY SPECIFIC GENERA
###################################

#physeq_filtered.rel = transform_sample_counts(physeq_filtered, function(x) x/sum(x)*100)

#some_Genus <- physeq_filtered.rel
#some_Genus <- tax_glom(some_Genus, "Genus")
#tax_table(some_Genus)[,6]

#some_Genus <- subset_taxa(some_Genus, Genus == "Prevotella"| Genus == "Veillonella" 
                     #   |Genus == "Blautia" |
                    #      Genus == "Streptococcus"| Genus == "Faecalibacterium" |
                    #      Genus == "Roseburia" | Genus == "Coprococcus" |
                    #      Genus == "Veillonella" | Genus == "Butyricicoccus" |
                    #      Genus == "Eubacterium" | Genus == "[Eubacterium] hallii group" | 
                    #     Genus == "Butyrivibrio" | Genus == "Lachnospira" |
                    #      Genus == "Haemophilus" | Genus == "Akkermansia" |
                    #     Genus == "Erysipelatoclostridium" | Genus == "Roseburia" | Genus== "Elusimicrobium"
                    #     )
#some_Genus <- psmelt(some_Genus)

# Plot  
# x = "", color ="", , facet~wrap and comparison list can be modify
pdf("Selected_Genus_NO_SCALE_Sample_type_DETAILED_FACET_TREATMENT.pdf", width = 60, height = 40)
ggplot(some_Genus, aes(x = Sample_type_DETAILED, y = Abundance)) +
  geom_boxplot() +
  #geom_point(aes(color = TREATMENT)) +
  geom_jitter(aes(colour = CONCENTRATION), shape=16,size = 2, position=position_jitter(0.2)) +
  #ylim(0, 5) +
  facet_wrap(TREATMENT~Genus, ncol = 12) +
  theme_bw() +
  theme(text = element_text(size = 20)) +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = -80, hjust = 0, vjust=0.5)) +
  ggtitle("Relative abundance of selected Genus") +
  ylab("Relative abundance")
dev.off()


# Plot  
# x = "", color ="", , facet~wrap and comparison list can be modify
pdf("Selected_Genus_MAX_10_ABUND_Sample_type_DETAILED_FACET_TREATMENT.pdf", width = 60, height = 40)
ggplot(some_Genus, aes(x = Sample_type_DETAILED, y = Abundance)) +
  geom_boxplot() +
  #geom_point(aes(color = TREATMENT)) +
  geom_jitter(aes(colour = CONCENTRATION), shape=16,size = 2, position=position_jitter(0.2)) +
  ylim(0, 10) +
  facet_wrap(TREATMENT~Genus, ncol = 12) +
  theme_bw() +
  theme(text = element_text(size = 20)) +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = -80, hjust = 0, vjust=0.5)) +
  ggtitle("Relative abundance of selected Genus") +
  ylab("Relative abundance")
dev.off()



# Plot  
# x = "", color ="", , facet~wrap and comparison list can be modify
pdf("Selected_Genus_MAX_5_ABUND_Sample_type_DETAILED_FACET_TREATMENT.pdf", width = 60, height = 40)
ggplot(some_Genus, aes(x = Sample_type_DETAILED, y = Abundance)) +
  geom_boxplot() +
  #geom_point(aes(color = TREATMENT)) +
  geom_jitter(aes(colour = CONCENTRATION), shape=16,size = 2, position=position_jitter(0.2)) +
  ylim(0, 5) +
  facet_wrap(TREATMENT~Genus, ncol = 12) +
  theme_bw() +
  theme(text = element_text(size = 20)) +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = -80, hjust = 0, vjust=0.5)) +
  ggtitle("Relative abundance of selected Genus") +
  ylab("Relative abundance")
dev.off()



# Plot  
# x = "", color ="", , facet~wrap and comparison list can be modify
pdf("Selected_Genus_MAX_1_ABUND_Sample_type_DETAILED_FACET_TREATMENT.pdf", width = 60, height = 40)
ggplot(some_Genus, aes(x = Sample_type_DETAILED, y = Abundance)) +
  geom_boxplot() +
  #geom_point(aes(color = TREATMENT)) +
  geom_jitter(aes(colour = CONCENTRATION), shape=16,size = 2, position=position_jitter(0.2)) +
  ylim(0, 1) +
  facet_wrap(TREATMENT~Genus, ncol = 12) +
  theme_bw() +
  theme(text = element_text(size = 20)) +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = -80, hjust = 0, vjust=0.5)) +
  ggtitle("Relative abundance of selected Genus") +
  ylab("Relative abundance")
dev.off()



###################################
#END OF TEST PLOT ONLY SPECIFIC Genus Sample_type_DETAILED FACET TREATMENT
###################################












###################################
#MAASLIN2 TEST
###################################

df_input_metadata = read.table(file             = "Metadata.txt", 
                               header           = TRUE, 
                               sep              = "\t", 
                               row.names        = 1,
                               stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
#View(df_input_metadata)




#Preparing the data


# Phylum Level

physeq_filtered_rel_abund_Phylum <- physeq_filtered %>% aggregate_taxa(level = "Phylum") %>% microbiome::transform(transform = "compositional")

otu_table_physeq_filtered_rel_abund_Phylum  <- otu_table(physeq_filtered_rel_abund_Phylum)

# Order Level


physeq_filtered_rel_abund_Order <- physeq_filtered %>% aggregate_taxa(level = "Order") %>% microbiome::transform(transform = "compositional")

otu_table_physeq_filtered_rel_abund_Order  <- otu_table(physeq_filtered_rel_abund_Order)

# CLass level


physeq_filtered_rel_abund_Class <- physeq_filtered %>% aggregate_taxa(level = "Class") %>% microbiome::transform(transform = "compositional")

otu_table_physeq_filtered_rel_abund_Class  <- otu_table(physeq_filtered_rel_abund_Class)

#Family level 

physeq_filtered_rel_abund_Family <- physeq_filtered %>% aggregate_taxa(level = "Family") %>% microbiome::transform(transform = "compositional")

otu_table_physeq_filtered_rel_abund_Family  <- otu_table(physeq_filtered_rel_abund_Family)

# Genus level

physeq_filtered_rel_abund_Genus <- physeq_filtered %>% aggregate_taxa(level = "Genus") %>% microbiome::transform(transform = "compositional")

otu_table_physeq_filtered_rel_abund_Genus  <- otu_table(physeq_filtered_rel_abund_Genus)


# Species level

physeq_filtered_rel_abund_Species <- physeq_filtered %>% aggregate_taxa(level = "Species") %>% microbiome::transform(transform = "compositional")

otu_table_physeq_filtered_rel_abund_Species  <- otu_table(physeq_filtered_rel_abund_Species)



#Phylum level
fit_data = Maaslin2(input_data     = otu_table_physeq_filtered_rel_abund_Phylum, 
                     input_metadata = df_input_metadata, 
                     min_prevalence = 0.1,
                     min_abundance  = 0.0001,
                     normalization  = "NONE",
                     output         = "Maaslin2_Analysis_Sample_type_Phylum", 
                     fixed_effects  = c("Sample_type"),
                     reference      = c("Sample_type,CTRL"),
                     random_effects = c("TREATMENT"),
                     cores=10)

# Order level

fit_data = Maaslin2(input_data     = otu_table_physeq_filtered_rel_abund_Order, 
                     input_metadata = df_input_metadata, 
                     min_prevalence = 0.1,
                     min_abundance  = 0.0001,
                     normalization  = "NONE",
                     output         = "Maaslin2_Analysis_Sample_type_Order", 
                     fixed_effects  = c("Sample_type"),
                     reference      = c("Sample_type,CTRL"),
                     random_effects = c("TREATMENT"),
                     cores=10)

# Class Level


fit_data = Maaslin2(input_data     = otu_table_physeq_filtered_rel_abund_Class, 
                     input_metadata = df_input_metadata, 
                     min_prevalence = 0.1,
                     min_abundance  = 0.0001,
                     normalization  = "NONE",
                     output         = "Maaslin2_Analysis_Sample_type_Class", 
                     fixed_effects  = c("Sample_type"),
                     reference      = c("Sample_type,CTRL"),
                     random_effects = c("TREATMENT"),
                     cores=10)


# Family Level



fit_data = Maaslin2(input_data     = otu_table_physeq_filtered_rel_abund_Family, 
                     input_metadata = df_input_metadata, 
                     min_prevalence = 0.1,
                     min_abundance  = 0.0001,
                     normalization  = "NONE",
                     output         = "Maaslin2_Analysis_Sample_type_Family", 
                     fixed_effects  = c("Sample_type"),
                     reference      = c("Sample_type,CTRL"),
                     random_effects = c("TREATMENT"),
                     cores=10)

# Genus Level


fit_data = Maaslin2(input_data     = otu_table_physeq_filtered_rel_abund_Genus, 
                     input_metadata = df_input_metadata, 
                     min_prevalence = 0.1,
                     min_abundance  = 0.0001,
                     normalization  = "NONE",
                     output         = "Maaslin2_Analysis_Sample_type_Genus", 
                     fixed_effects  = c("Sample_type"),
                     reference      = c("Sample_type,CTRL"),
                     random_effects = c("TREATMENT"),
                     cores=10)


# Sample_type Species Level

fit_data = Maaslin2(input_data     = otu_table_physeq_filtered_rel_abund_Species, 
                     input_metadata = df_input_metadata, 
                     min_prevalence = 0.1,
                     min_abundance  = 0.0001,
                     normalization  = "NONE",
                     output         = "Maaslin2_Analysis_Sample_type_Species", 
                     fixed_effects  = c("Sample_type"),
                     reference      = c("Sample_type,CTRL"),
                     random_effects = c("TREATMENT"),
                     cores=10)


#BILE_STATES ANALYSIS

#Phylum level
fit_data = Maaslin2(input_data     = otu_table_physeq_filtered_rel_abund_Phylum, 
                     input_metadata = df_input_metadata, 
                     min_prevalence = 0.1,
                     min_abundance  = 0.0001,
                     normalization  = "NONE",
                     output         = "Maaslin2_Analysis_BILE_STATES_Phylum", 
                     fixed_effects  = c("BILE_STATES"),
                     reference      = c("BILE_STATES,CTRL"),
                     random_effects = c("TREATMENT"),
                     cores=10)



# Order Level


fit_data = Maaslin2(input_data     = otu_table_physeq_filtered_rel_abund_Order, 
                     input_metadata = df_input_metadata, 
                     min_prevalence = 0.1,
                     min_abundance  = 0.0001,
                     normalization  = "NONE",
                     output         = "Maaslin2_Analysis_BILE_STATES_Order", 
                     fixed_effects  = c("BILE_STATES"),
                     reference      = c("BILE_STATES,CTRL"),
                     random_effects = c("TREATMENT"),
                     cores=10)

# Class Level


fit_data = Maaslin2(input_data     = otu_table_physeq_filtered_rel_abund_Class, 
                     input_metadata = df_input_metadata, 
                     min_prevalence = 0.1,
                     min_abundance  = 0.0001,
                     normalization  = "NONE",
                     output         = "Maaslin2_Analysis_BILE_STATES_Class", 
                     fixed_effects  = c("BILE_STATES"),
                     reference      = c("BILE_STATES,CTRL"),
                     random_effects = c("TREATMENT"),
                     cores=10)


# Family Level


fit_data = Maaslin2(input_data     = otu_table_physeq_filtered_rel_abund_Family, 
                     input_metadata = df_input_metadata, 
                     min_prevalence = 0.1,
                     min_abundance  = 0.0001,
                     normalization  = "NONE",
                     output         = "Maaslin2_Analysis_BILE_STATES_Family", 
                     fixed_effects  = c("BILE_STATES"),
                     reference      = c("BILE_STATES,CTRL"),
                     random_effects = c("TREATMENT"),
                     cores=10)

# Genus Level


fit_data = Maaslin2(input_data     = otu_table_physeq_filtered_rel_abund_Genus, 
                     input_metadata = df_input_metadata, 
                     min_prevalence = 0.1,
                     min_abundance  = 0.0001,
                     normalization  = "NONE",
                     output         = "Maaslin2_Analysis_BILE_STATES_Genus", 
                     fixed_effects  = c("BILE_STATES"),
                     reference      = c("BILE_STATES,CTRL"),
                     random_effects = c("TREATMENT"),
                     cores=10)


# BILE_STATES Species Level


fit_data = Maaslin2(input_data     = otu_table_physeq_filtered_rel_abund_Species, 
                     input_metadata = df_input_metadata, 
                     min_prevalence = 0.1,
                     min_abundance  = 0.0001,
                     normalization  = "NONE",
                     output         = "Maaslin2_Analysis_BILE_STATES_Species", 
                     fixed_effects  = c("BILE_STATES"),
                     reference      = c("BILE_STATES,CTRL"),
                     random_effects = c("TREATMENT"),
                     cores=10)

# Sample_type_DETAILED


#Phylum level
fit_data = Maaslin2(input_data     = otu_table_physeq_filtered_rel_abund_Phylum, 
                     input_metadata = df_input_metadata, 
                     min_prevalence = 0.1,
                     min_abundance  = 0.0001,
                     normalization  = "NONE",
                     output         = "Maaslin2_Analysis_Sample_type_DETAILED_Phylum", 
                     fixed_effects  = c("Sample_type_DETAILED"),
                     reference      = c("Sample_type_DETAILED,CTRL"),
                     random_effects = c("TREATMENT"),
                     cores=10)

# Order level

fit_data = Maaslin2(input_data     = otu_table_physeq_filtered_rel_abund_Order, 
                     input_metadata = df_input_metadata, 
                     min_prevalence = 0.1,
                     min_abundance  = 0.0001,
                     normalization  = "NONE",
                     output         = "Maaslin2_Analysis_Sample_type_DETAILED_Order", 
                     fixed_effects  = c("Sample_type_DETAILED"),
                     reference      = c("Sample_type_DETAILED,CTRL"),
                     random_effects = c("TREATMENT"),
                     cores=10)

# Class Level


fit_data = Maaslin2(input_data     = otu_table_physeq_filtered_rel_abund_Class, 
                     input_metadata = df_input_metadata, 
                     min_prevalence = 0.1,
                     min_abundance  = 0.0001,
                     normalization  = "NONE",
                     output         = "Maaslin2_Analysis_Sample_type_DETAILED_Class", 
                     fixed_effects  = c("Sample_type_DETAILED"),
                     reference      = c("Sample_type_DETAILED,CTRL"),
                     random_effects = c("TREATMENT"),
                     cores=10)


# Family Level



fit_data = Maaslin2(input_data     = otu_table_physeq_filtered_rel_abund_Family, 
                     input_metadata = df_input_metadata, 
                     min_prevalence = 0.1,
                     min_abundance  = 0.0001,
                     normalization  = "NONE",
                     output         = "Maaslin2_Analysis_Sample_type_DETAILED_Family", 
                     fixed_effects  = c("Sample_type_DETAILED"),
                     reference      = c("Sample_type_DETAILED,CTRL"),
                     random_effects = c("TREATMENT"),
                     cores=10)

# Genus Level


fit_data = Maaslin2(input_data     = otu_table_physeq_filtered_rel_abund_Genus, 
                     input_metadata = df_input_metadata, 
                     min_prevalence = 0.1,
                     min_abundance  = 0.0001,
                     normalization  = "NONE",
                     output         = "Maaslin2_Analysis_Sample_type_DETAILED_Genus", 
                     fixed_effects  = c("Sample_type_DETAILED"),
                     reference      = c("Sample_type_DETAILED,CTRL"),
                     random_effects = c("TREATMENT"),
                     cores=10)


# Sample_type_DETAILED Species Level

fit_data = Maaslin2(input_data     = otu_table_physeq_filtered_rel_abund_Species, 
                     input_metadata = df_input_metadata, 
                     min_prevalence = 0.1,
                     min_abundance  = 0.0001,
                     normalization  = "NONE",
                     output         = "Maaslin2_Analysis_Sample_type_DETAILED_Species", 
                     fixed_effects  = c("Sample_type_DETAILED"),
                     reference      = c("Sample_type_DETAILED,CTRL"),
                     random_effects = c("TREATMENT"),
                     cores=10)




###################################
# END OF MAASLIN2 TEST
###################################



##################################################

#HEATMAP OF ABUNDANCE (BUT ONLY PRESENCE/ABSENCE)!!!


# make a baTREATMENT heatmap
#pdf("BATREATMENT_PRESENCE_ABSENCE_HEATMAP_ALL_SAMPLES.pdf", width = 20, height=20)
pdf("BATREATMENT_HEATMAP_ALL_SAMPLES.pdf")

phyloseq::plot_heatmap(physeq_filtered, "PCoA", "bray", "TREATMENT", low="#66CCFF", high="#000033", na.value="white", title = "PCoA-Bray")
dev.off()



#I WILL TRY TO DO A NORMAL HEAT MAP, BUT COLOR TOTAL BLACK WHEN HIGHER THAN ZERO!! GENUS


# Relative abundance for each group
vb_1 <- subset_samples(physeq_filtered)

# Percentage
vb_1 <- transform_sample_counts(vb_1, function(x) 100*x/sum(x))

# Agglomerate as a taxonomic rank
vb_1 <- tax_glom(vb_1, "Genus")

# Prune top X genera (only show top x values)
vb_1 <- prune_taxa(names(sort(taxa_sums(vb_1), TRUE)[1:100]), vb_1)

# Merging subest phyloseq - Genus level agglomeration
physeq_vb <- tax_glom(vb_1, "Genus")

pdf("physeq_filtered_heat_final_REL_ABUND_PHYLUM_TEST_2_Genus.pdf", width = 30, height = 10)

phyloseq::plot_heatmap(physeq_vb, "NMDS", "jaccard", "Sample_type", low="#66CCFF", high="#000033", na.value="white", title = "NMDS-jaccard", "Genus")
dev.off()

pdf("physeq_filtered_heat_final_REL_ABUND_PHYLUM_TEST_3_Genus.pdf", width = 30, height = 10)

phyloseq::plot_heatmap(physeq_vb, "NMDS", "jaccard", "TREATMENT", low="#66CCFF", high="#000033", na.value="white", title = "NMDS-jaccard", "Genus")
dev.off()





#I WILL TRY TO DO A NORMAL HEAT MAP, BUT COLOR TOTAL BLACK WHEN HIGHER THAN ZERO!! CLASS


# Relative abundance for each group
vb_1 <- subset_samples(physeq_filtered)

# Percentage
vb_1 <- transform_sample_counts(vb_1, function(x) 100*x/sum(x))

# Agglomerate as a taxonomic rank
vb_1 <- tax_glom(vb_1, "Class")

# Prune top X genera (only show top x values)
vb_1 <- prune_taxa(names(sort(taxa_sums(vb_1), TRUE)[1:100]), vb_1)

# Merging subest phyloseq - Class level agglomeration
physeq_vb <- tax_glom(vb_1, "Class")

pdf("physeq_filtered_heat_final_REL_ABUND_PHYLUM_TEST_2_Class.pdf", width = 30, height = 10)

phyloseq::plot_heatmap(physeq_vb, "NMDS", "jaccard", "Sample_type", low="#66CCFF", high="#000033", na.value="white", title = "NMDS-jaccard", "Class")
dev.off()

pdf("physeq_filtered_heat_final_REL_ABUND_PHYLUM_TEST_3_Class.pdf", width = 30, height = 10)

phyloseq::plot_heatmap(physeq_vb, "NMDS", "jaccard", "TREATMENT", low="#66CCFF", high="#000033", na.value="white", title = "NMDS-jaccard", "Class")
dev.off()



#I WILL TRY TO DO A NORMAL HEAT MAP, BUT COLOR TOTAL BLACK WHEN HIGHER THAN ZERO!! PHYLUM


# Relative abundance for each group
vb_1 <- subset_samples(physeq_filtered)

# Percentage
vb_1 <- transform_sample_counts(vb_1, function(x) 100*x/sum(x))

# Agglomerate as a taxonomic rank
vb_1 <- tax_glom(vb_1, "Phylum")

# Prune top X genera (only show top x values)
vb_1 <- prune_taxa(names(sort(taxa_sums(vb_1), TRUE)[1:100]), vb_1)

# Merging subest phyloseq - Phylum level agglomeration
physeq_vb <- tax_glom(vb_1, "Phylum")

pdf("physeq_filtered_heat_final_REL_ABUND_PHYLUM_TEST_2_Phylum.pdf", width = 30, height = 10)

phyloseq::plot_heatmap(physeq_vb, "NMDS", "jaccard", "TREATMENT", low="#66CCFF", high="#000033", na.value="white", title = "NMDS-jaccard", "Phylum") +
theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = -80, hjust = 0, vjust=0.5)) 
dev.off()







#################################################
#################################################
#################################################



#################################################




##################### TEST JUL RELATIVE ABUND working
###### PHYLUM

physeq_filtered.rel = transform_sample_counts(physeq_filtered, function(OTU) (OTU/sum(OTU))*100)


get_taxa(physeq_filtered.rel, sample_names(physeq)[100])


physeq_filtered.rel = transform_sample_counts(physeq_filtered, function(OTU) (OTU/sum(OTU))*100)
# agglomerate taxa
glom <- tax_glom(physeq_filtered.rel, taxrank = 'Phylum', NArm = FALSE)


physeq_filtered.melt <- psmelt(glom)


physeq_filtered.melt <- physeq_filtered.melt %>%
  group_by(TREATMENT,Sample_type,Phylum) %>%
  mutate(median=median(Abundance))
# select group median > 1
keep <- unique(physeq_filtered.melt$Phylum[physeq_filtered.melt$median > 1])
physeq_filtered.melt$Phylum[!(physeq_filtered.melt$Phylum %in% keep)] <- "< 1%"
#to get the same rows together
physeq_filtered.melt_sum <- physeq_filtered.melt %>%
  group_by(Sample_type, TREATMENT, CONCENTRATION, Phylum) %>%
  summarise(Abundance=sum(Abundance)/3)


# change to character for easy-adjusted level
physeq_filtered.melt_sum$Phylum <- as.character(physeq_filtered.melt_sum$Phylum)



#View(physeq_filtered.melt.test)

pdf("BarPlot_RelativeAbundance_TREATMENTCOMs_Phylum_Sample_type_V2.pdf",  width=20, height=60)

ggplot(physeq_filtered.melt_sum, aes(x = CONCENTRATION, y = Abundance)) + 
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", aes(fill=Phylum)) + 
  labs(x="", y="%") +
  facet_wrap(TREATMENT ~ Sample_type, scales= "free_x", ncol=5) +
 theme_classic() + 
  theme(strip.background = element_blank(), 
        axis.text.x.bottom = element_text(angle = -90))
dev.off()












######CLASS

physeq_filtered.rel = transform_sample_counts(physeq_filtered, function(x) x/sum(x)*100)
# agglomerate taxa
glom <- tax_glom(physeq_filtered.rel, taxrank = 'Class', NArm = FALSE)
physeq_filtered.melt <- psmelt(glom)
# change to character for easy-adjusted level
physeq_filtered.melt$Class <- as.character(physeq_filtered.melt$Class)

physeq_filtered.melt <- physeq_filtered.melt %>%
  group_by(Sample_type, Class) %>%
  mutate(median=median(Abundance))
# select group median > 1
keep <- unique(physeq_filtered.melt$Class[physeq_filtered.melt$median > 1])
physeq_filtered.melt$Class[!(physeq_filtered.melt$Class %in% keep)] <- "< 1%"
#to get the same rows together
physeq_filtered.melt_sum <- physeq_filtered.melt %>%
  group_by(TREATMENT,Sample_type,Class) %>%
  summarise(Abundance=sum(Abundance))

pdf("BarPlot_RelativeAbundance_TREATMENTCOMs_Class_Sample_type_V2.pdf",  width=20, height=10)

ggplot(physeq_filtered.melt_sum, aes(x = TREATMENT, y = Abundance, fill = Class)) + 
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", aes(fill=Class)) + 
  labs(x="", y="%") +
  facet_wrap(TREATMENT~Sample_type, scales= "free_x", nrow=1) +
 theme_classic() + 
  theme(strip.background = element_blank(), 
        axis.text.x.bottom = element_text(angle = -90))
dev.off()



######FAMILY

pdf("BarPlot_RelativeAbundance_TREATMENTCOMs_Family_Sample_type_V2.pdf",  width=20, height=10)
physeq_filtered.rel = transform_sample_counts(physeq_filtered, function(x) x/sum(x)*100)
# agglomerate taxa
glom <- tax_glom(physeq_filtered.rel, taxrank = 'Family', NArm = FALSE)
physeq_filtered.melt <- psmelt(glom)
# change to character for easy-adjusted level
physeq_filtered.melt$Family <- as.character(physeq_filtered.melt$Family)

physeq_filtered.melt <- physeq_filtered.melt %>%
  group_by(Sample_type, Family) %>%
  mutate(median=median(Abundance))
# select group median > 1
keep <- unique(physeq_filtered.melt$Family[physeq_filtered.melt$median > 1])
physeq_filtered.melt$Family[!(physeq_filtered.melt$Family %in% keep)] <- "< 1%"
#to get the same rows together
physeq_filtered.melt_sum <- physeq_filtered.melt %>%
  group_by(TREATMENT,Sample_type,Family) %>%
  summarise(Abundance=sum(Abundance))

ggplot(physeq_filtered.melt_sum, aes(x = TREATMENT, y = Abundance, fill = Family)) + 
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", aes(fill=Family)) + 
  labs(x="", y="%") +
  facet_wrap(TREATMENT~Sample_type, scales= "free_x", nrow=1) +
 theme_classic() + 
  theme(strip.background = element_blank(), 
        axis.text.x.bottom = element_text(angle = -90))
dev.off()



######GENUS

pdf("BarPlot_RelativeAbundance_TREATMENTCOMs_Genus_Sample_type_V2.pdf",  width=20, height=10)
physeq_filtered.rel = transform_sample_counts(physeq_filtered, function(x) x/sum(x)*100)
# agglomerate taxa
glom <- tax_glom(physeq_filtered.rel, taxrank = 'Genus', NArm = FALSE)
physeq_filtered.melt <- psmelt(glom)
# change to character for easy-adjusted level
physeq_filtered.melt$Genus <- as.character(physeq_filtered.melt$Genus)

physeq_filtered.melt <- physeq_filtered.melt %>%
  group_by(Sample_type, Genus) %>%
  mutate(median=median(Abundance))
# select group median > 1
keep <- unique(physeq_filtered.melt$Genus[physeq_filtered.melt$median > 1])
physeq_filtered.melt$Genus[!(physeq_filtered.melt$Genus %in% keep)] <- "< 1%"
#to get the same rows together
physeq_filtered.melt_sum <- physeq_filtered.melt %>%
  group_by(TREATMENT,Sample_type,Genus) %>%
  summarise(Abundance=sum(Abundance))

ggplot(physeq_filtered.melt_sum, aes(x = TREATMENT, y = Abundance, fill = Genus)) + 
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", aes(fill=Genus)) + 
  labs(x="", y="%") +
  facet_wrap(TREATMENT~Sample_type, scales= "free_x", nrow=1) +
 theme_classic() + 
  theme(strip.background = element_blank(), 
        axis.text.x.bottom = element_text(angle = -90))
dev.off()



######SPECIES

pdf("BarPlot_RelativeAbundance_TREATMENTCOMs_Species_Sample_type_V2.pdf",  width=20, height=10)
physeq_filtered.rel = transform_sample_counts(physeq_filtered, function(x) x/sum(x)*100)
# agglomerate taxa
glom <- tax_glom(physeq_filtered.rel, taxrank = 'Species', NArm = FALSE)
physeq_filtered.melt <- psmelt(glom)
# change to character for easy-adjusted level
physeq_filtered.melt$Species <- as.character(physeq_filtered.melt$Species)

physeq_filtered.melt <- physeq_filtered.melt %>%
  group_by(Sample_type, Species) %>%
  mutate(median=median(Abundance))
# select group median > 1
keep <- unique(physeq_filtered.melt$Species[physeq_filtered.melt$median > 1])
physeq_filtered.melt$Species[!(physeq_filtered.melt$Species %in% keep)] <- "< 1%"
#to get the same rows together
physeq_filtered.melt_sum <- physeq_filtered.melt %>%
  group_by(TREATMENT,Sample_type,Species) %>%
  summarise(Abundance=sum(Abundance))

ggplot(physeq_filtered.melt_sum, aes(x = TREATMENT, y = Abundance, fill = Species)) + 
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", aes(fill=Species)) + 
  labs(x="", y="%") +
  facet_wrap(TREATMENT~Sample_type, scales= "free_x", nrow=1) +
 theme_classic() + 
  theme(strip.background = element_blank(), 
        axis.text.x.bottom = element_text(angle = -90))
dev.off()






#######################



#Make a genus vs log2FC plot of the significant OTUs:

#Adding pseudo-count of one to avoid problems with zero in Deseq analysis

# Create a copy of the physeq_filtered that we want to keep intact

physeq_filtered_copy <- physeq_filtered

View(physeq_filtered_copy)

otu_table(physeq_filtered_copy) <- otu_table(physeq_filtered_copy) + 1

#View(otu_table(physeq_filtered_copy))

sample_data(physeq_filtered_copy)$Sample_type <- as.factor(sample_data(physeq_filtered_copy)$Sample_type)

#Convert the phyloseq object to a DESeqDataSet and run DESeq2:

ds = phyloseq_to_deseq2(physeq_filtered_copy, ~ Sample_type)

#adding a pseudcount for aving no zero

# The next step takes a bit of time
ds = DESeq(ds)
View(ds)
#Extract the result table from the ds object usind the DESeq2 function results and filter the OTUs using a False Discovery Rate (FDR) cutoff of 0.01. In this example we return the significantly differentially abundant OTU between the seasons “Spring” and “Fall”:


rm(res_sig)
alpha = 0.05
res = results(ds, contrast=c("Sample_type", "Lithocholic acid", "CTRL"), alpha=alpha)
res = res[order(res$padj, na.last=NA), ]
res_sig = res[(res$padj < alpha), ]
res_sig


res_sig = cbind(as(res_sig, "data.frame"), as(tax_table(physeq_filtered_copy)[rownames(res_sig), ], "matrix"))


View(res_sig)


#### To order the graph according to log2foldchange Genus level

x = tapply(res_sig$log2FoldChange, res_sig$Genus, function(x) max(x))
x = sort(x, TRUE)
res_sig$Genus = factor(as.character(res_sig$Genus), levels=names(x))


pdf("Genus_vs_log2FC_significant_OTUs_TREATMENT_vs_FECES.pdf",  width=20, height=10)
ggplot(res_sig, aes(x=Genus, y=log2FoldChange, color=Class)) +
    geom_jitter(size=3, width = 0.2) +
    theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = -90, hjust = 0, vjust=0.5))
dev.off()



#######################

######################

# DESeq2 Differential abundance analysis with heatmap (https://www.yanh.org/2021/01/01/microbiome-r/)
#### GENUS level

sample_data(physeq_filtered_copy)$Sample_type <- as.factor(sample_data(physeq_filtered_copy)$Sample_type) # factorize for DESeq2

physeq_filtered_copy.taxa <- tax_glom(physeq_filtered_copy, taxrank = 'Genus', NArm = TRUE)


# pairwise comparison between BILE_STATES and CONTROL
physeq_filtered_copy.taxa.sub <- subset_samples(physeq_filtered_copy.taxa, Sample_type %in% c("Lithocholic acid", "CTRL"))
# filter sparse features, with > 90% zeros
physeq_filtered_copy.taxa.pse.sub <- prune_taxa(rowSums(otu_table(physeq_filtered_copy.taxa.sub) == 0) < ncol(otu_table(physeq_filtered_copy.taxa.sub)) * 0.9, physeq_filtered_copy.taxa.sub)
physeq_filtered_copy_ds = phyloseq_to_deseq2(physeq_filtered_copy.taxa.pse.sub, ~ Sample_type)
# use alternative estimator on a TREATMENT of "every gene contains a sample with a zero"
ds <- estimateSizeFactors(physeq_filtered_copy_ds, type="poscounts")
ds = DESeq(ds, test="Wald", fitType="parametric")
alpha = 0.05 
res = results(ds, alpha=alpha)
res = res[order(res$padj, na.last=NA), ]
taxa_sig = rownames(res[1:40, ]) # select bottom 40 with lowest p.adj values
physeq_filtered_copy.taxa.rel <- transform_sample_counts(physeq_filtered_copy, function(x) x/sum(x)*100)
physeq_filtered_copy.taxa.rel.sig <- prune_taxa(taxa_sig, physeq_filtered_copy.taxa.rel)
# Only keep "Lithocholic acid", "CTRL" samples
physeq_filtered_copy.taxa.rel.sig <- prune_samples(colnames(otu_table(physeq_filtered_copy.taxa.pse.sub)), physeq_filtered_copy.taxa.rel.sig)



matrix <- as.matrix(data.frame(otu_table(physeq_filtered_copy.taxa.rel.sig)))
rownames(matrix) <- as.character(tax_table(physeq_filtered_copy.taxa.rel.sig)[, "Genus"])
metadata_sub <- data.frame(sample_data(physeq_filtered_copy.taxa.rel.sig))
# Define the annotation color for columns and rows
annotation_col = data.frame(
    BILE_STATES = as.factor(metadata_sub$BILE_STATES), 
    `Sample_type` = as.factor(metadata_sub$Sample_type), 
    check.names = FALSE
)
rownames(annotation_col) = rownames(metadata_sub)

annotation_row = data.frame(
    Class = as.factor(tax_table(physeq_filtered_copy.taxa.rel.sig)[, "Class"])
)
rownames(annotation_row) = rownames(matrix)

# ann_color should be named vectors
Class_col = RColorBrewer::brewer.pal(length(levels(annotation_row$Class)), "Paired")
names(Class_col) = levels(annotation_row$Class)
ann_colors = list(
    `Sample_type` = c("CTRL" = "orange", "Lithocholic acid" = "brown"),
    Class = Class_col
)


pdf("DESeq2_Differential_Abundance_analysis_Genus_CTRL_vs_Lithocholic.pdf",  width=15, height=10)
ComplexHeatmap::pheatmap(matrix, scale= "row", 
                         annotation_col = annotation_col, 
                         annotation_row = annotation_row, 
                         annotation_colors = ann_colors)
dev.off()



sample_data(physeq_filtered_copy)$Sample_type <- as.factor(sample_data(physeq_filtered_copy)$Sample_type) # factorize for DESeq2

physeq_filtered_copy.taxa <- tax_glom(physeq_filtered_copy, taxrank = 'Genus', NArm = TRUE)



# pairwise comparison between BILE_STATES and CONTROL
physeq_filtered_copy.taxa.sub <- subset_samples(physeq_filtered_copy.taxa, Sample_type %in% c("Chenodeoxycholic Acid", "CTRL"))
# filter sparse features, with > 90% zeros
physeq_filtered_copy.taxa.pse.sub <- prune_taxa(rowSums(otu_table(physeq_filtered_copy.taxa.sub) == 0) < ncol(otu_table(physeq_filtered_copy.taxa.sub)) * 0.9, physeq_filtered_copy.taxa.sub)
physeq_filtered_copy_ds = phyloseq_to_deseq2(physeq_filtered_copy.taxa.pse.sub, ~ Sample_type)
# use alternative estimator on a TREATMENT of "every gene contains a sample with a zero"
ds <- estimateSizeFactors(physeq_filtered_copy_ds, type="poscounts")
ds = DESeq(ds, test="Wald", fitType="parametric")
alpha = 0.05 
res = results(ds, alpha=alpha)
res = res[order(res$padj, na.last=NA), ]
taxa_sig = rownames(res[1:40, ]) # select bottom 40 with lowest p.adj values
physeq_filtered_copy.taxa.rel <- transform_sample_counts(physeq_filtered_copy, function(x) x/sum(x)*100)
physeq_filtered_copy.taxa.rel.sig <- prune_taxa(taxa_sig, physeq_filtered_copy.taxa.rel)
# Only keep "Chenodeoxycholic acid", "CTRL" samples
physeq_filtered_copy.taxa.rel.sig <- prune_samples(colnames(otu_table(physeq_filtered_copy.taxa.pse.sub)), physeq_filtered_copy.taxa.rel.sig)



matrix <- as.matrix(data.frame(otu_table(physeq_filtered_copy.taxa.rel.sig)))
rownames(matrix) <- as.character(tax_table(physeq_filtered_copy.taxa.rel.sig)[, "Genus"])
metadata_sub <- data.frame(sample_data(physeq_filtered_copy.taxa.rel.sig))
# Define the annotation color for columns and rows
annotation_col = data.frame(
    CONCENTRATION = as.factor(metadata_sub$CONCENTRATION), 
    `Sample_type` = as.factor(metadata_sub$Sample_type), 
    check.names = FALSE
)
rownames(annotation_col) = rownames(metadata_sub)

annotation_row = data.frame(
    Class = as.factor(tax_table(physeq_filtered_copy.taxa.rel.sig)[, "Class"])
)
rownames(annotation_row) = rownames(matrix)

# ann_color should be named vectors
Class_col = RColorBrewer::brewer.pal(length(levels(annotation_row$Class)), "Paired")
names(Class_col) = levels(annotation_row$Class)
ann_colors = list(
    `Sample_type` = c("CTRL" = "orange", "Chenodeoxycholic Acid" = "brown"),
    Class = Class_col
)


pdf("DESeq2_Differential_Abundance_analysis_Genus_CTRL_vs_Chenodeoxycholic.pdf",  width=15, height=10)
ComplexHeatmap::pheatmap(matrix, scale= "row", 
                         annotation_col = annotation_col, 
                         annotation_row = annotation_row, 
                         annotation_colors = ann_colors)
dev.off()




####################################################





```{r}
# Checking for relative abundances of salivarius vs Enterococcus bacteria (boxplots)


# Load required libraries
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(viridis)
library(ggbreak)  # For breaking the y-axis

# Import data
physeq_data <- read.table("physeq_filtered_relative_abundance.all.tsv", 
                          sep = "\t", header = TRUE, stringsAsFactors = FALSE)

# Convert ASV column to rownames if necessary
rownames(physeq_data) <- physeq_data$ASV

# Filter for Enterococcus and Salivarius, excluding NA species
filtered_data <- physeq_data %>%
  filter((Species == "salivarius" | Genus == "Enterococcus") & !is.na(Species)) %>%
  select(Species, Genus, starts_with("LYS_")) %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = starts_with("LYS_"), 
               names_to = "Condition", values_to = "Abundance") %>%
  mutate(Abundance = ifelse(Abundance == 0, 0.0001, Abundance))  # Replace 0 with pseudocount

# Get unique Enterococcus species
enterococcus_species <- filtered_data %>%
  filter(Genus == "Enterococcus") %>%
  pull(Species) %>%
  unique()

# Open PDF for output
pdf("Paired_Salivarius_Enterococcus_Boxplots.pdf", width = 8, height = 6)

# Loop through each Enterococcus species and create a separate plot
for (species in enterococcus_species) {
  
  # Subset data for the current Enterococcus species and Salivarius
  plot_data <- filtered_data %>%
    filter(Species %in% c(species, "salivarius")) %>%
    mutate(Species = factor(Species, levels = c(species, "salivarius")))  # Ensure Salivarius is always on the right

  # Create a Sample_ID column to group by matching samples
  plot_data$Sample_ID <- plot_data$Condition

  # Generate plot
  p <- ggplot(plot_data, aes(x = Species, y = Abundance, fill = Species)) +
    geom_boxplot(alpha = 0.7, outlier.shape = NA) +  # Boxplots (all samples included)
    geom_jitter(aes(group = Sample_ID), shape = 16, position = position_jitter(0.2), alpha = 0.6) +  # Data points
    geom_line(aes(group = Sample_ID, color = Sample_ID), linewidth = 0.8, alpha = 0.7) +  # Connecting lines
    theme_minimal() +
    theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1, vjust = 1)) +
    labs(title = paste("Abundance of", species, "vs Salivarius"),
         x = "Bacteria", y = "Relative Abundance (%)") +
    scale_fill_viridis_d() +  # Automatically assigns colors
    scale_color_viridis_d(guide = "none")   # Use same color scheme for lines, hide legend

  # Check if y-axis needs to be broken for better visualization
  max_y <- max(plot_data$Abundance, na.rm = TRUE)
  if (max_y > 50) {
    p <- p + scale_y_break(c(5, 50))  # Break between 5 and 50 if large differences exist
  }

  # Print the plot to the PDF
  print(p)
}

# Close PDF file
dev.off()



```





```{r}

#### USING the phyloseq object with SIC8 and SIC9 (excluding SIC10) instead of importing the tables

# Load required libraries
library(phyloseq)
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(viridis)

# Extract OTU table and convert to dataframe
otu_df <- as.data.frame(otu_table(physeq_glom_physeq_filtered_SIC8_SIC9_NO_EB_24_48_SPECIES_ra))

# Extract taxonomy and metadata
tax_df <- as.data.frame(tax_table(physeq_glom_physeq_filtered_SIC8_SIC9_NO_EB_24_48_SPECIES_ra))
meta_df <- as.data.frame(sample_data(physeq_glom_physeq_filtered_SIC8_SIC9_NO_EB_24_48_SPECIES_ra))

# Add taxonomic information to the OTU table
otu_df$Species <- tax_df$Species  

# Pivot the OTU table to long format (keeping `fastqID` as sample identifier)
long_data <- otu_df %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = -Species, names_to = "fastqID", values_to = "Abundance")

# Merge with metadata using `fastqID`
long_data <- left_join(long_data, meta_df, by = "fastqID")

# Filter for only *Salivarius* and *Coprococcus comes*
filtered_data <- long_data %>%
  filter(Species %in% c("salivarius", "comes") & !is.na(Species)) %>%
  mutate(Abundance = ifelse(Abundance == 0, 0.0001, Abundance))  

# Ensure every `fastqID` has both species before plotting
paired_data <- filtered_data %>%
  group_by(fastqID) %>%
  filter(n_distinct(Species) == 2) %>%  
  ungroup()

# Convert from long to wide format for correct line plotting
wide_data <- paired_data %>%
  select(fastqID, Species, Abundance, TREATMENT) %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = Species, values_from = Abundance) %>%
  drop_na()  

# Ensure correct column names
colnames(wide_data) <- c("fastqID", "TREATMENT", "Coprococcus_comes", "Salivarius")

# Open PDF for output (multiple pages)
pdf("Connected_Boxplots_Salivarius_CoprococcusComes.pdf", width = 10, height = 6)

# First Page: Full-Scale Boxplot
p1 <- ggplot(paired_data, aes(x = Species, y = Abundance, fill = Species)) +
  geom_boxplot(alpha = 0.7, outlier.shape = NA) +  
  geom_jitter(aes(color = TREATMENT), shape = 16, position = position_jitter(0.2), alpha = 0.6) +  
  geom_line(aes(group = fastqID, color = TREATMENT), alpha = 0.5) +
  scale_x_discrete(labels = c("Coprococcus comes", "Salivarius")) +  
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1, vjust = 1)) +
  labs(title = "Paired Abundance of Coprococcus comes vs Salivarius",
       x = "Bacteria", y = "Relative Abundance (%)", color = "Treatment") +
  scale_fill_viridis_d() +  
  scale_color_manual(values = c("Lysin" = "#E69F00", "NO_LYSIN" = "#56B4E9"))  

print(p1)  # Print first page

# Second Page: Zoomed-In View (Max Y = 5%)
p2 <- ggplot(paired_data, aes(x = Species, y = Abundance, fill = Species)) +
  geom_boxplot(alpha = 0.7, outlier.shape = NA) +  
  geom_jitter(aes(color = TREATMENT), shape = 16, position = position_jitter(0.2), alpha = 0.6) +  
  geom_line(aes(group = fastqID, color = TREATMENT), alpha = 0.5) +
  scale_x_discrete(labels = c("Coprococcus comes", "Salivarius")) +  
  ylim(0, 5) +  # Set y-axis limit to 5%
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1, vjust = 1)) +
  labs(title = "Zoomed-In: Low Abundance (Below 5%) - Coprococcus comes vs Salivarius",
       x = "Bacteria", y = "Relative Abundance (%)", color = "Treatment") +
  scale_fill_viridis_d() +  
  scale_color_manual(values = c("Lysin" = "#E69F00", "NO_LYSIN" = "#56B4E9"))  

print(p2)  # Print second page

# Close PDF file
dev.off()


```



# Load required libraries
library(phyloseq)
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(viridis)

# Extract OTU table and convert to dataframe
otu_df <- as.data.frame(otu_table(physeq_glom_physeq_filtered_SIC8_SIC9_NO_EB_24_48_SPECIES_ra))

# Extract taxonomy and metadata
tax_df <- as.data.frame(tax_table(physeq_glom_physeq_filtered_SIC8_SIC9_NO_EB_24_48_SPECIES_ra))
meta_df <- as.data.frame(sample_data(physeq_glom_physeq_filtered_SIC8_SIC9_NO_EB_24_48_SPECIES_ra))

# Add taxonomic information to the OTU table
otu_df$Species <- tax_df$Species  
otu_df$Genus <- tax_df$Genus  

# Pivot the OTU table to long format (keeping `fastqID` as sample identifier)
long_data <- otu_df %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = -c(Species, Genus), names_to = "fastqID", values_to = "Abundance")

# Merge with metadata using `fastqID`
long_data <- left_join(long_data, meta_df, by = "fastqID")

# Filter for *Salivarius* and all *Enterococcus* species
filtered_data <- long_data %>%
  filter(Species == "salivarius" | Genus == "Enterococcus") %>%
  mutate(Abundance = ifelse(Abundance == 0, 0.0001, Abundance))  

# Get unique Enterococcus species
enterococcus_species <- filtered_data %>%
  filter(Genus == "Enterococcus") %>%
  pull(Species) %>%
  unique()

# Open multi-page PDF for output
pdf("Connected_Boxplots_Salivarius_vs_Enterococcus.pdf", width = 10, height = 6)

# Loop through each Enterococcus species
for (species in enterococcus_species) {
  
  # Subset data for the current Enterococcus species and *Salivarius*
  paired_data <- filtered_data %>%
    filter(Species %in% c(species, "salivarius")) %>%
    group_by(fastqID) %>%
    filter(n_distinct(Species) == 2) %>%  # Ensure both species are present
    ungroup()
  
  # Convert from long to wide format for correct line plotting
  wide_data <- paired_data %>%
    select(fastqID, Species, Abundance, TREATMENT) %>%
    pivot_wider(names_from = Species, values_from = Abundance) %>%
    drop_na()  

  # Rename columns for clarity
  colnames(wide_data) <- c("fastqID", "TREATMENT", "Enterococcus", "Salivarius")

  # First Page: Full-Scale Boxplot
  p1 <- ggplot(paired_data, aes(x = Species, y = Abundance, fill = Species)) +
    geom_boxplot(alpha = 0.7, outlier.shape = NA) +  
    geom_jitter(aes(color = TREATMENT), shape = 16, position = position_jitter(0.2), alpha = 0.6) +  
    geom_line(aes(group = fastqID, color = TREATMENT), alpha = 0.5) +
    scale_x_discrete(labels = c(species, "Salivarius")) +  
    theme_minimal() +
    theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1, vjust = 1)) +
    labs(title = paste("Paired Abundance of", species, "vs Salivarius"),
         x = "Bacteria", y = "Relative Abundance (%)", color = "Treatment") +
    scale_fill_viridis_d() +  
    scale_color_manual(values = c("Lysin" = "#E69F00", "NO_LYSIN" = "#56B4E9"))  

  print(p1)  # Print first page

  # Second Page: Zoomed-In View (Max Y = 5%)
  low_abundance_data <- paired_data %>% filter(Abundance <= 5)

  p2 <- ggplot(low_abundance_data, aes(x = Species, y = Abundance, fill = Species)) +
    geom_boxplot(alpha = 0.7, outlier.shape = NA) +  
    geom_jitter(aes(color = TREATMENT), shape = 16, position = position_jitter(0.2), alpha = 0.6) +  
    geom_line(aes(group = fastqID, color = TREATMENT), alpha = 0.5) +
    scale_x_discrete(labels = c(species, "Salivarius")) +  
    ylim(0, 5) +  # Correctly limit y-axis to 5%
    theme_minimal() +
    theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1, vjust = 1)) +
    labs(title = paste("Zoomed-In: Low Abundance (≤5%) -", species, "vs Salivarius"),
         x = "Bacteria", y = "Relative Abundance (%)", color = "Treatment") +
    scale_fill_viridis_d() +  
    scale_color_manual(values = c("Lysin" = "#E69F00", "NO_LYSIN" = "#56B4E9"))  

  print(p2)  # Print second page
}

# Close PDF file
dev.off()






##########################
## TEST COMPARATIVE BOXPLOT FOR S COMES
##########################


# Load libraries
library(phyloseq)
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(readr)

# STEP 1: Subset phyloseq (exclude EB, keep 24 & 48h)
physeq_filtered_NO_EB <- subset_samples(physeq_filtered, TREATMENT != "EB")
physeq_filtered_NO_EB_24_48 <- subset_samples(physeq_filtered_NO_EB, TIME %in% c("24", "48"))

# STEP 2: Transform to relative abundance
physeq_ra <- transform_sample_counts(physeq_filtered_NO_EB_24_48, function(x) x / sum(x))

# STEP 3: Extract OTU, taxonomy, and metadata
otu_df <- as.data.frame(otu_table(physeq_ra))
tax_df <- as.data.frame(tax_table(physeq_ra))
meta_df <- as.data.frame(sample_data(physeq_ra))

# STEP 4: Filter for comes and salivarius
tax_df$ASV <- rownames(tax_df)
filtered_tax <- tax_df %>%
  filter((Genus == "Coprococcus" & Species == "comes") |
         (Genus == "Streptococcus" & Species == "salivarius"))

# STEP 5: Subset OTU table and convert to long format
otu_filtered <- otu_df[rownames(otu_df) %in% filtered_tax$ASV, , drop = FALSE]
otu_filtered$ASV <- rownames(otu_filtered)

otu_long <- otu_filtered %>%
  pivot_longer(-ASV, names_to = "fastqID", values_to = "Abundance") %>%
  left_join(filtered_tax[, c("ASV", "Genus", "Species")], by = "ASV") %>%
  mutate(Taxon = paste(Genus, Species, sep = " "))

# STEP 6: Aggregate to one value per sample per taxon
otu_summary <- otu_long %>%
  group_by(fastqID, Taxon) %>%
  summarise(Abundance = sum(Abundance), .groups = "drop")

# STEP 7: Pivot to wide format and remove samples with both taxa absent
otu_wide <- otu_summary %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = Taxon, values_from = Abundance, values_fill = 0) %>%
  filter(`Coprococcus comes` > 0 | `Streptococcus salivarius` > 0)

# STEP 8: Add pseudocount
pseudo <- 0.0001
otu_wide <- otu_wide %>%
  mutate(
    `Coprococcus comes` = `Coprococcus comes` + pseudo,
    `Streptococcus salivarius` = `Streptococcus salivarius` + pseudo
  )

# STEP 9: Merge with metadata and add SIC_TIME
final_table <- left_join(otu_wide, meta_df, by = "fastqID") %>%
  mutate(SIC_TIME = paste0(SIC_ID, " | ", TIME, "h"))

# STEP 10: Compute direction of change per sample
line_data <- final_table %>%
  mutate(delta = `Coprococcus comes` - `Streptococcus salivarius`,
         direction = ifelse(delta > 0, "Up", "Down")) %>%
  select(fastqID, direction)

# STEP 11: Reshape for plotting and join direction
plot_data <- final_table %>%
  select(fastqID, TREATMENT, SIC_TIME, `Coprococcus comes`, `Streptococcus salivarius`) %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = c(`Coprococcus comes`, `Streptococcus salivarius`),
               names_to = "Taxon", values_to = "Abundance") %>%
  mutate(
    Taxon = factor(Taxon, levels = c("Coprococcus comes", "Streptococcus salivarius")),
    TREATMENT = factor(TREATMENT, levels = c("NO_LYSIN", "Lysin"))
  ) %>%
  left_join(line_data, by = "fastqID")
  
# Adjust Taxon order and SIC_TIME order


plot_data <- plot_data %>%
  mutate(
    Taxon = factor(Taxon, levels = c("Streptococcus salivarius", "Coprococcus comes")),
    SIC_TIME = factor(SIC_TIME, levels = c(
      "SIC8A | 24h", "SIC8A | 48h",
      "SIC9A | 24h", "SIC9A | 48h",
      "SIC10A | 24h", "SIC10A | 48h"
    ))
  )


# Build final plot
p <- ggplot(plot_data, aes(x = Taxon, y = Abundance)) +
  geom_line(aes(group = fastqID, color = direction), alpha = 0.6, linewidth = 0.5) +
  geom_boxplot(aes(fill = Taxon), outlier.shape = NA, alpha = 0.3,
               position = position_dodge(width = 0.75)) +
  geom_point(color = "black",
             position = position_dodge(width = 0.75),
             alpha = 0.5, size = 1.5) +
  scale_color_manual(
    values = c("Up" = "#ff7f0e", "Down" = "#1f77b4"),
    name = "Line direction"
  ) +
  scale_fill_manual(
    values = c(
      "Streptococcus salivarius" = "#1f77b4",
      "Coprococcus comes" = "#ff7f0e"
    )
  ) +
  scale_y_log10() +
  facet_grid(SIC_TIME ~ TREATMENT, switch = "y", scales = "free_y", space = "free") +
  theme_classic(base_size = 11) +
  labs(
    title = "Paired Abundance with Direction-Colored Lines (log10)",
    x = "Taxon",
    y = "Relative Abundance (%) [log10]",
    fill = "Taxon"
  ) +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
    strip.text.y.left = element_text(angle = 0, size = 10),
    strip.placement = "outside",
    panel.spacing.y = unit(1.5, "lines"),
    legend.position = "top"
  )

# Show and export
print(p)
ggsave("paired_boxplot_custom_colors_faceted_ordered_log10.pdf", plot = p, width = 12, height = 8)


##################### OTHER GRAPH

# Set factor levels
plot_data <- plot_data %>%
  mutate(
    Taxon = factor(Taxon, levels = c("Streptococcus salivarius", "Coprococcus comes")),
    TREATMENT = factor(TREATMENT, levels = c("NO_LYSIN", "Lysin"))
  )

# Styled and simplified plot
p <- ggplot(plot_data, aes(x = Taxon, y = Abundance)) +
  geom_line(aes(group = fastqID, color = direction), alpha = 0.6, linewidth = 0.5) +
  geom_boxplot(aes(fill = Taxon), outlier.shape = NA, alpha = 0.3,
               position = position_dodge(width = 0.75)) +
  geom_point(color = "black",
             position = position_dodge(width = 0.75),
             alpha = 0.5, size = 1.5) +
  scale_color_manual(
    values = c("Up" = "#ff7f0e", "Down" = "#1f77b4"),
    name = "Line direction"
  ) +
  scale_fill_manual(
    values = c(
      "Streptococcus salivarius" = "#1f77b4",
      "Coprococcus comes" = "#ff7f0e"
    )
  ) +
  scale_y_log10() +
  facet_wrap(~ TREATMENT, nrow = 1) +
  theme_classic(base_size = 11) +
  labs(
    title = "Paired Abundance by Treatment with Direction-Colored Lines (log10)",
    x = "Taxon",
    y = "Relative Abundance (%) [log10]",
    fill = "Taxon"
  ) +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
    strip.text = element_text(size = 10),
    legend.position = "top"
  )

# Print and export
print(p)
ggsave("paired_boxplot_TREATMENT_only_styled.pdf", plot = p, width = 8, height = 5)







##########################
## TEST COMPARATIVE BOXPLOT FOR ANY 2 SPECIES OF CHOICE
##########################


# Load libraries
library(phyloseq)
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(readr)

# STEP 1: Subset phyloseq (exclude EB, keep 24 & 48h)
physeq_filtered_NO_EB <- subset_samples(physeq_filtered, TREATMENT != "EB")
physeq_filtered_NO_EB_24_48 <- subset_samples(physeq_filtered_NO_EB, TIME %in% c("24", "48"))

# STEP 2: Transform to relative abundance
physeq_ra <- transform_sample_counts(physeq_filtered_NO_EB_24_48, function(x) x / sum(x))

# STEP 3: Extract OTU, taxonomy, and metadata
otu_df <- as.data.frame(otu_table(physeq_ra))
tax_df <- as.data.frame(tax_table(physeq_ra))
meta_df <- as.data.frame(sample_data(physeq_ra))

# STEP 4: Filter for comes and salivarius
tax_df$ASV <- rownames(tax_df)
filtered_tax <- tax_df %>%
  filter((Genus == "Coprococcus" & Species == "comes") |
         (Genus == "Streptococcus" & Species == "salivarius"))

# STEP 5: Subset OTU table and convert to long format
otu_filtered <- otu_df[rownames(otu_df) %in% filtered_tax$ASV, , drop = FALSE]
otu_filtered$ASV <- rownames(otu_filtered)

otu_long <- otu_filtered %>%
  pivot_longer(-ASV, names_to = "fastqID", values_to = "Abundance") %>%
  left_join(filtered_tax[, c("ASV", "Genus", "Species")], by = "ASV") %>%
  mutate(Taxon = paste(Genus, Species, sep = " "))

# STEP 6: Aggregate to one value per sample per taxon
otu_summary <- otu_long %>%
  group_by(fastqID, Taxon) %>%
  summarise(Abundance = sum(Abundance), .groups = "drop")

# STEP 7: Pivot to wide format and remove samples with both taxa absent
otu_wide <- otu_summary %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = Taxon, values_from = Abundance, values_fill = 0) %>%
  filter(`Coprococcus comes` > 0 | `Streptococcus salivarius` > 0)

# STEP 8: Add pseudocount
pseudo <- 0.0001
otu_wide <- otu_wide %>%
  mutate(
    `Coprococcus comes` = `Coprococcus comes` + pseudo,
    `Streptococcus salivarius` = `Streptococcus salivarius` + pseudo
  )

# STEP 9: Merge with metadata and add SIC_TIME
final_table <- left_join(otu_wide, meta_df, by = "fastqID") %>%
  mutate(SIC_TIME = paste0(SIC_ID, " | ", TIME, "h"))

# STEP 10: Compute direction of change per sample
line_data <- final_table %>%
  mutate(delta = `Coprococcus comes` - `Streptococcus salivarius`,
         direction = ifelse(delta > 0, "Up", "Down")) %>%
  select(fastqID, direction)

# STEP 11: Reshape for plotting and join direction
plot_data <- final_table %>%
  select(fastqID, TREATMENT, SIC_TIME, `Coprococcus comes`, `Streptococcus salivarius`) %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = c(`Coprococcus comes`, `Streptococcus salivarius`),
               names_to = "Taxon", values_to = "Abundance") %>%
  mutate(
    Taxon = factor(Taxon, levels = c("Coprococcus comes", "Streptococcus salivarius")),
    TREATMENT = factor(TREATMENT, levels = c("NO_LYSIN", "Lysin"))
  ) %>%
  left_join(line_data, by = "fastqID")
  
# Adjust Taxon order and SIC_TIME order


plot_data <- plot_data %>%
  mutate(
    Taxon = factor(Taxon, levels = c("Streptococcus salivarius", "Coprococcus comes")),
    SIC_TIME = factor(SIC_TIME, levels = c(
      "SIC8A | 24h", "SIC8A | 48h",
      "SIC9A | 24h", "SIC9A | 48h",
      "SIC10A | 24h", "SIC10A | 48h"
    ))
  )


# Build final plot
p <- ggplot(plot_data, aes(x = Taxon, y = Abundance)) +
  geom_line(aes(group = fastqID, color = direction), alpha = 0.6, linewidth = 0.5) +
  geom_boxplot(aes(fill = Taxon), outlier.shape = NA, alpha = 0.3,
               position = position_dodge(width = 0.75)) +
  geom_point(color = "black",
             position = position_dodge(width = 0.75),
             alpha = 0.5, size = 1.5) +
  scale_color_manual(
    values = c("Up" = "#ff7f0e", "Down" = "#1f77b4"),
    name = "Line direction"
  ) +
  scale_fill_manual(
    values = c(
      "Streptococcus salivarius" = "#1f77b4",
      "Coprococcus comes" = "#ff7f0e"
    )
  ) +
  scale_y_log10() +
  facet_grid(SIC_TIME ~ TREATMENT, switch = "y", scales = "free_y", space = "free") +
  theme_classic(base_size = 11) +
  labs(
    title = "Paired Abundance with Direction-Colored Lines (log10)",
    x = "Taxon",
    y = "Relative Abundance (%) [log10]",
    fill = "Taxon"
  ) +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
    strip.text.y.left = element_text(angle = 0, size = 10),
    strip.placement = "outside",
    panel.spacing.y = unit(1.5, "lines"),
    legend.position = "top"
  )

# Show and export
print(p)
ggsave("paired_boxplot_custom_colors_faceted_ordered_log10.pdf", plot = p, width = 12, height = 8)


##################### OTHER GRAPH

# Set factor levels
plot_data <- plot_data %>%
  mutate(
    Taxon = factor(Taxon, levels = c("Streptococcus salivarius", "Coprococcus comes")),
    TREATMENT = factor(TREATMENT, levels = c("NO_LYSIN", "Lysin"))
  )

# Styled and simplified plot
p <- ggplot(plot_data, aes(x = Taxon, y = Abundance)) +
  geom_line(aes(group = fastqID, color = direction), alpha = 0.6, linewidth = 0.5) +
  geom_boxplot(aes(fill = Taxon), outlier.shape = NA, alpha = 0.3,
               position = position_dodge(width = 0.75)) +
  geom_point(color = "black",
             position = position_dodge(width = 0.75),
             alpha = 0.5, size = 1.5) +
  scale_color_manual(
    values = c("Up" = "#ff7f0e", "Down" = "#1f77b4"),
    name = "Line direction"
  ) +
  scale_fill_manual(
    values = c(
      "Streptococcus salivarius" = "#1f77b4",
      "Coprococcus comes" = "#ff7f0e"
    )
  ) +
  scale_y_log10() +
  facet_wrap(~ TREATMENT, nrow = 1) +
  theme_classic(base_size = 11) +
  labs(
    title = "Paired Abundance by Treatment with Direction-Colored Lines (log10)",
    x = "Taxon",
    y = "Relative Abundance (%) [log10]",
    fill = "Taxon"
  ) +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
    strip.text = element_text(size = 10),
    legend.position = "top"
  )

# Print and export
print(p)
ggsave("paired_boxplot_TREATMENT_only_styled.pdf", plot = p, width = 8, height = 5)






##########################
## COMPARATIVE BOXPLOT FOR ANY 2 SPECIES OF CHOICE
##########################

# Load libraries
library(phyloseq)
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(readr)


You can :

print(unique(tax_df$Genus)) 
#and
unique(tax_df$Species) to inspect available values.

# OR
# If you want to list the species available for a specific genus of interest, change the genus_of_interest by
# the genus you want to know more about

View(
  tax_df %>%
    filter(Genus == "Bacteroides")
)



# ===== Choose your two species of interest =====
species_1 <- c("Coprococcus", "comes")
species_2 <- c("Streptococcus", "salivarius")

# ===== Create species label for file names =====
species_label_1 <- paste(species_1, collapse = "_")
species_label_2 <- paste(species_2, collapse = "_")
plot_tag <- paste0(species_label_1, "_vs_", species_label_2)

# STEP 1: Subset phyloseq (exclude EB, keep 24 & 48h)
physeq_filtered_NO_EB <- subset_samples(physeq_filtered, TREATMENT != "EB")
physeq_filtered_NO_EB_24_48 <- subset_samples(physeq_filtered_NO_EB, TIME %in% c("24", "48"))

# STEP 2: Transform to relative abundance
physeq_ra <- transform_sample_counts(physeq_filtered_NO_EB_24_48, function(x) x / sum(x))

# STEP 3: Extract OTU, taxonomy, and metadata
otu_df <- as.data.frame(otu_table(physeq_ra))
tax_df <- as.data.frame(tax_table(physeq_ra))
meta_df <- as.data.frame(sample_data(physeq_ra))

# STEP 4: Filter for selected species
tax_df$ASV <- rownames(tax_df)
filtered_tax <- tax_df %>%
  filter((Genus == species_1[1] & Species == species_1[2]) |
         (Genus == species_2[1] & Species == species_2[2]))

# STEP 5: Subset OTU table and convert to long format
otu_filtered <- otu_df[rownames(otu_df) %in% filtered_tax$ASV, , drop = FALSE]
otu_filtered$ASV <- rownames(otu_filtered)

otu_long <- otu_filtered %>%
  pivot_longer(-ASV, names_to = "fastqID", values_to = "Abundance") %>%
  left_join(filtered_tax[, c("ASV", "Genus", "Species")], by = "ASV") %>%
  mutate(Taxon = paste(Genus, Species, sep = " "))

# STEP 6: Aggregate to one value per sample per taxon
otu_summary <- otu_long %>%
  group_by(fastqID, Taxon) %>%
  summarise(Abundance = sum(Abundance), .groups = "drop")

# STEP 7: Pivot to wide format and remove samples with both taxa absent
taxon_names <- unique(otu_summary$Taxon)
otu_wide <- otu_summary %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = Taxon, values_from = Abundance, values_fill = 0) %>%
  filter((.data[[taxon_names[1]]] > 0) | (.data[[taxon_names[2]]] > 0))

# STEP 8: Add pseudocount
pseudo <- 0.0001
otu_wide[[taxon_names[1]]] <- otu_wide[[taxon_names[1]]] + pseudo
otu_wide[[taxon_names[2]]] <- otu_wide[[taxon_names[2]]] + pseudo

# STEP 9: Merge with metadata and add SIC_TIME
final_table <- left_join(otu_wide, meta_df, by = "fastqID") %>%
  mutate(SIC_TIME = paste0(SIC_ID, " | ", TIME, "h"))

# STEP 10: Compute direction of change per sample
line_data <- final_table %>%
  mutate(delta = .data[[taxon_names[1]]] - .data[[taxon_names[2]]],
         direction = ifelse(delta > 0, "Up", "Down")) %>%
  select(fastqID, direction)

# STEP 11: Reshape for plotting and join direction
plot_data <- final_table %>%
  select(fastqID, TREATMENT, SIC_TIME, all_of(taxon_names)) %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = all_of(taxon_names),
               names_to = "Taxon", values_to = "Abundance") %>%
  mutate(
    Taxon = factor(Taxon, levels = taxon_names),
    TREATMENT = factor(TREATMENT, levels = c("NO_LYSIN", "Lysin"))
  ) %>%
  left_join(line_data, by = "fastqID")

# Export underlying data
write_tsv(plot_data, paste0("plot_data_", plot_tag, ".tsv"))

# === Plot 1: Faceted by SIC_TIME & TREATMENT ===
p1 <- ggplot(plot_data, aes(x = Taxon, y = Abundance)) +
  geom_line(aes(group = fastqID, color = direction), alpha = 0.6, linewidth = 0.5) +
  geom_boxplot(aes(fill = Taxon), outlier.shape = NA, alpha = 0.3,
               position = position_dodge(width = 0.75)) +
  geom_point(color = "black",
             position = position_dodge(width = 0.75),
             alpha = 0.5, size = 1.5) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("Up" = "#ff7f0e", "Down" = "#1f77b4"), name = "Line direction") +
  scale_y_log10() +
  facet_grid(SIC_TIME ~ TREATMENT, switch = "y", scales = "free_y", space = "free") +
  theme_classic(base_size = 11) +
  labs(
    title = paste("Paired Abundance:", species_label_1, "vs", species_label_2),
    x = "Taxon",
    y = "Relative Abundance (%) [log10]",
    fill = "Taxon"
  ) +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
    strip.text.y.left = element_text(angle = 0, size = 10),
    strip.placement = "outside",
    panel.spacing.y = unit(1.5, "lines"),
    legend.position = "top"
  )

ggsave(
  paste0("paired_boxplot_faceted_", plot_tag, ".pdf"),
  plot = p1, width = 12, height = 8
)

# === Plot 2: Faceted by TREATMENT only ===
p2 <- ggplot(plot_data, aes(x = Taxon, y = Abundance)) +
  geom_line(aes(group = fastqID, color = direction), alpha = 0.6, linewidth = 0.5) +
  geom_boxplot(aes(fill = Taxon), outlier.shape = NA, alpha = 0.3,
               position = position_dodge(width = 0.75)) +
  geom_point(color = "black",
             position = position_dodge(width = 0.75),
             alpha = 0.5, size = 1.5) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("Up" = "#ff7f0e", "Down" = "#1f77b4"), name = "Line direction") +
  scale_y_log10() +
  facet_wrap(~ TREATMENT, nrow = 1) +
  theme_classic(base_size = 11) +
  labs(
    title = paste("Abundance by Treatment:", species_label_1, "vs", species_label_2),
    x = "Taxon",
    y = "Relative Abundance (%) [log10]",
    fill = "Taxon"
  ) +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
    strip.text = element_text(size = 10),
    legend.position = "top"
  )

ggsave(
  paste0("paired_boxplot_TREATMENT_only_", plot_tag, ".pdf"),
  plot = p2, width = 8, height = 5
)







###################### EXTRACTING SPECIES LEVEL INFO TO BE USED IN SPIECEASY FOR CO-ABUNDANCE ANALYSES

library(phyloseq)
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)

# Transform to relative abundance
physeq_species <- tax_glom(physeq, taxrank = "Species")
physeq_ra <- transform_sample_counts(physeq_species, function(x) x / sum(x))

# Get taxonomy and OTU table
tax_df <- as.data.frame(tax_table(physeq_ra))
otu_df <- as.data.frame(otu_table(physeq_ra))

# Append species names to OTU IDs
tax_df$Species_full <- paste(tax_df$Genus, tax_df$Species, sep = " ")
tax_df$Species_full[is.na(tax_df$Species)] <- NA  # Remove malformed names

# Remove NAs
valid_species <- tax_df[!is.na(tax_df$Species_full), ]
otu_filtered <- otu_df[rownames(otu_df) %in% rownames(valid_species), ]
rownames(otu_filtered) <- valid_species$Species_full[match(rownames(otu_filtered), rownames(valid_species))]

# Transpose to samples × species
spiec_input <- t(otu_filtered)

# Save table for verification
write.table(spiec_input, file = "spiec_species_input.tsv", sep = "\t", quote = FALSE)


# Transpose species × samples
fastspar_input <- t(spiec_input)

# Replace NA with pseudocounts
fastspar_input[is.na(fastspar_input)] <- 0.0001

# Set correct header and rownames
fastspar_df <- as.data.frame(fastspar_input)
fastspar_df <- tibble::rownames_to_column(fastspar_df, var = "#OTU ID")

# Write to TSV with BIOM format header
write.table(fastspar_df, file = "fastspar_input.tsv", sep = "\t", quote = FALSE, row.names = FALSE)
